{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2003 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2003poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 56th Cannes Film Festival featuring an original illustration by [[Jenny Holzer]].{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2003/posters/off.html |title=Posters 2003 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214071102/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2003/posters/off.html |archive-date=14 December 2013 |df=dmy}} | host = [[Monica Bellucci]] | number = 20 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[Fanfan la Tulipe (2003 film)|Fanfan la Tulipe]]'' | closing = ''[[Charlie: The Life and Art of Charles Chaplin|Charlie: The Life and Art
of Charles Chaplin]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Elephant (2003 film)|Elephant]]'' | date = {{Start date|2003|05|14|df=y}} – {{End date|2003|05|25|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2002 Cannes Film Festival|2002]] | next = [[2004 Cannes Film Festival|2004]] }} The '''56th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 14 to 25 May 2003. French opera and theatre director, filmmaker, actor and producer [[Patrice Chéreau]] was the President of the Jury for the main competition.{{cite web |title=56ème Festival International du Film - Cannes |url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2003.htm |access-date=21 June 2017 |work=cinema-francais.fr |language=fr}} [[Monica Bellucci]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{cite news |title=‘Fanfan,’ Bellucci to open Cannes fest |url=https://variety.com/2003/film/news/fanfan-bellucci-to-open-cannes-fest-1117884568/ |access-date=25 May 2017 |work=Variety.com}} American filmmaker [[Gus Van Sant]] won the ''[[Palme d'Or]]'', the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[Elephant (2003 film)|Elephant]]'', based on the [[Columbine High School massacre]]. [[Ingmar Bergman]] was awarded with the [[Honorary Palme d'Or]] for his contributions to cinema throughout his career.{{cite news |date=25 May 2003 |title=Cannes winners in full |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/2937274.stm |access-date=25 May 2017 |work=BBC News}}{{cite news |title=A disappointing Cannes finishes with controversial decisions |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2003/may/26/cannes2003.cannesfilmfestival |access-date=25 May 2017 |work=theguardian.com}}{{cite news |last1=Scott |first1=A.O. |date=June 1, 2003 |title=FILM; Remembering Cannes 2003: Worst Festival Ever |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2003/06/01/movies/film-remembering-cannes-2003-worst-festival-ever.html |access-date=15 November 2023 |work=The New York Times}} The festival opened with ''[[Fanfan la Tulipe (2003 film)|Fanfan la Tulipe]]'' by [[Gérard Krawczyk]], ''and closed with [[Charlie: The Life and Art of Charles Chaplin]]'' by [[Richard Schickel]].{{cite news |url=http://www.urbancinefile.com.au/home/view.asp?a=7383&s=features |title=Cannes Film Festival 2003 - Preview |work=urbancinefile.com.au |access-date=25 May 2017 |archive-date=14 December 2013 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214053022/http://www.urbancinefile.com.au/home/view.asp?a=7383&s=features |url-status=dead }} [[File:Un certain regard 03.jpg|thumb|261x261px|2003 [[Un Certain Regard]] poster, adapted from an original illustration by ''Oswaldo Vigas''.{{cite news|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2003/posters/ucr.html
